description_long: This is a rustic, two-story wooden saloon with a prominent front facade featuring a large overhanging sign that reads 'SALOON' in bold, Western-style lettering. The building has a wide, covered porch supported by wooden posts and railings, with a balcony above that mirrors the porch's design. The exterior is constructed from weathered wooden planks, giving it an aged, Old West appearance. Large, rectangular windows with dark frames are symmetrically arranged on both floors, providing a sense of balance and openness. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with reddish-brown shingles, while the sides and rear of the building are plain, lacking decorative elements. The overall design evokes the classic American frontier saloon, often found in historic Western towns.
description: A two-story, weathered wooden saloon with a prominent front sign, covered porch, balcony, and symmetrical windows, evoking a classic Old West style.
description_view_0: The front view shows the saloon's main entrance, covered porch, balcony, and the large 'SALOON' sign above two sets of double windows.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the plain wooden siding and two rows of large, rectangular windows, with the porch and balcony visible at the front edge.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a simple, unadorned wooden wall with no windows or doors, and the pitched roof extending to the back.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the same window arrangement and plain wooden siding, with the porch and balcony at the front.
attribute_signage: A large, rectangular, wall-mounted sign above the main entrance on the front facade, with the word 'SALOON' in bold, Western-style lettering on a yellowed background.
attribute_entrance_design: One main entrance located at the center of the front facade, accessible via a covered wooden porch. The entrance appears to be a double swinging door, typical of Western saloons, framed by wooden posts and railings, with a balcony above.
attribute_window_arrangement: Eight large, rectangular windows with dark frames are symmetrically arranged on the front and both sides, four per floor, providing a balanced and open appearance. No windows are visible on the rear.
attribute_architectural_style: Rustic Western, characterized by weathered wood construction, a prominent front sign, covered porch, balcony, and symmetrical window arrangement typical of American frontier saloons.
